facts.primaryTitle: The Smiling Man Followed Her Home
facts.setting: The man danced beneath a streetlight with a smile too wide for his face.
criticalEvents: The man danced beneath a streetlight with a smile too wide for his face.
When Elena crossed the road, he folded sideways and followed.
Elena Brooks was a nurse walking home after a late shift when the story began in empty city streets shortly before dawn.
The first reports did not describe an obvious monster or violent attack.
facts.threat: The man danced beneath a streetlight with a smile too wide for his face.
facts.primaryReveal: The final warning is therefore simple: when the same impossible detail appears twice, do not wait for a third occurrence to prove that it is real.
facts.finalConsequence: The final warning is therefore simple: when the same impossible detail appears twice, do not wait for a third occurrence to prove that it is real.
facts.unresolvedQuestion: Did Elena Brooks defeat the thing at the centre of the story, or did the encounter simply teach it how to remain hidden?
